key_highlights: Global markets plunge amid concerns about aftermath of China summit, with no progress on Iran and few deals from Trump's trip.
Bond selloff intensifies, threatening to knock AI stock frenzy off course and resetting Fed rate-cut odds.
AI chipmaker Cerebras soars 90% in year's biggest IPO, while retail euphoria turns six-week fund into record-busting AI trade.
Record drone attack on Moscow kills three and targets refinery, escalating geopolitical risks.
WHO declares Congo Ebola outbreak a global health emergency, with rare strain and no vaccine linked to 87 deaths.
Berkshire boosted Alphabet stake, bought Delta Air, sold Visa and Mastercard; Pershing Square snapped up Microsoft, exited Hilton, slashed Alphabet.
Trump calls China trip 'historic moment' but ambiguity over Taiwan and lack of Iran progress fuel sell-off.
Bond yields rise sharply, mortgage rates rise, and high-yield savings accounts offer up to 4.1% APY.
Starbucks cuts 300 corporate jobs as turnaround continues; DraftKeyes eyes prediction markets as next evolution.
Long Island Rail workers strike for first time since 1994, stranding 300,000 daily riders.

sectors.trend: Extreme divergence: AI stocks like Cerebras (+90% IPO) and BigBear.ai (+4% post-earnings) surge, but bond selloff threatens to derail the AI frenzy. Retail euphoria drives record inflows into AI-focused funds, while Wall Street warns of late-1990s extremes. Pershing Square and Berkshire show mixed positioning—Microsoft bought, Alphabet trimmed by Pershing but added by Berkshire.
sectors.implications: High volatility expected; AI names may correct sharply if bond yields continue rising. Investors should consider taking profits on overextended AI plays and focus on companies with strong fundamentals and cash flows.

sectors.trend: Iran war shadow drives global inventory race; record drone attack on Moscow targets refinery; Vietnam refinery stable but supertanker halted by US Navy resumes. Oil prices likely elevated, benefiting energy stocks but pressuring consumers.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains a hedge against geopolitical risk. Refinery attacks and inventory hoarding suggest continued upward pressure on oil prices. Investors should overweight energy and defense.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates rise with Treasury yields; NNN REIT gets price target hike from Citi; CT Real Estate Investment Trust Q1 earnings call highlights. Housing market under pressure from higher rates, but REITs with strong fundamentals may offer value.
sectors.implications: Rising rates are negative for homebuilders and mortgage lenders, but well-managed REITs with long-term leases may be defensive. Consider selective REIT exposure.

sectors.trend: Chipotle stock outlook mixed; Starbucks cuts 300 jobs; subscription fatigue alienating consumers. Consumer spending remains resilient but shifting toward value and experience.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary names may face headwinds from inflation and rate hikes. Focus on companies with pricing power and strong brand loyalty.

sectors.trend: Bond selloff resets Fed rate-cut odds; public BDCs pricing in most pain since Covid; BlackRock private credit under federal probe. LPL Financial announces $125M buyback.
sectors.implications: Higher for longer rates benefit banks' net interest margins but hurt bond portfolios. Private credit faces regulatory scrutiny. Financials may be volatile but offer value for long-term investors.

sectors.trend: WHO declares Congo Ebola outbreak a global health emergency; rare strain with no vaccine linked to 87 deaths. Becton Dickinson delivers solid Q2 performance.
sectors.implications: Ebola outbreak could boost vaccine and diagnostic stocks. Healthcare remains defensive; BDX is a solid pick.

sectors.trend: Long Island Rail workers strike; Air India suspends ultra-long-haul routes; Malaysia unveils measures to help airlines hit by Iran war. MasTec backlog surges.
sectors.implications: Infrastructure spending and defense-related transport may benefit. Airlines face headwinds from fuel costs and labor disputes.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk escalation: Iran war, Russia drone attack, China-Taiwan ambiguity, Malaysia-Norway tensions.
Bond market turmoil: Yields surge, Fed rate-cut odds reset, risk rally threatened.
AI mania vs. reality: Record IPO, retail euphoria, but Wall Street warns of dot-com-like extremes.
Global health emergency: Ebola outbreak with no vaccine.
Central bank divergence: ECB modest rate hike vs. Fed pause uncertainty.
Corporate positioning shifts: Berkshire, Pershing Square, and other big investors rotate portfolios.
Labor unrest: LIRR strike, Starbucks job cuts.
Housing market strain: Rising mortgage rates, Australia tax changes.
Supply chain and inventory race: Oil, silver, and other commodities hoarding.
Regulatory scrutiny: BlackRock private credit, Chick-fil-A religious discrimination suit.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to defense, energy, and gold. Reduce exposure to emerging markets and sectors reliant on global trade.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Bond market selloff and rising yields
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in fixed income; favor floating-rate bonds and short-term Treasuries. Rebalance equity portfolio away from high-growth, high-duration stocks.
Take profits on overextended AI names; rotate to value and quality. Use options to hedge downside.
Monitor vaccine and diagnostic stocks; avoid travel-related sectors. Consider adding healthcare exposure.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Labor strikes and supply chain disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify supply chains; avoid stocks heavily dependent on affected transport routes. Consider infrastructure and logistics plays.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory risk in private credit and tech
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to BDCs and private credit funds. Monitor regulatory developments in AI and finance.
Avoid homebuilders and mortgage lenders; favor REITs with long-term leases and strong balance sheets.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy and defense stocks as geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: Iran war, Russia drone attacks, and global inventory race support higher oil prices and defense spending.

opportunities.recommendation: Add healthcare exposure, especially vaccine and diagnostic companies
opportunities.rationale: Ebola outbreak with no vaccine creates demand for rapid response; BDX solid earnings.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively buy high-quality AI stocks with strong fundamentals on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: AI trend is secular, but valuations are stretched. Cerebras IPO success shows appetite; focus on profitable AI plays.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in infrastructure and logistics companies
opportunities.rationale: Supply chain disruptions and inventory hoarding boost demand for logistics and infrastructure.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider high-yield savings and short-term bonds for cash allocation
opportunities.rationale: Rising yields offer attractive risk-free returns; 4.1% APY available.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth, high-duration tech and AI stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond selloff threatens to knock AI frenzy off course; valuations at dot-com extremes.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to value and dividend-paying stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising rates favor value over growth; dividends provide income cushion.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against geopolitical risk with gold and commodities
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war, Russia attacks, and global uncertainty boost safe-haven demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in fixed income; favor floating-rate notes
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond selloff likely to continue; floating-rate instruments protect against rising yields.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid sectors exposed to labor strikes and consumer weakness
defensive_moves.rationale: LIRR strike, Starbucks job cuts, subscription fatigue signal consumer stress.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Negative to neutral. Geopolitical risks (Iran, Russia, Taiwan) and bond selloff will dominate. AI stocks may correct sharply. Investors should focus on defensives, energy, and short-duration bonds. Volatility likely elevated.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously positive. AI secular trend intact but after correction. Central banks may ease if growth slows. Infrastructure spending and energy transition provide long-term opportunities. Ebola outbreak contained likely. Housing market may stabilize if rates peak.
key_catalysts: Fed rate decision and commentary on inflation and bond yields.
Progress or escalation in Iran conflict and Russia-Ukraine war.
China-Taiwan relations and Trump's next moves.
Ebola outbreak containment and vaccine development.
AI earnings reports from Nvidia, Microsoft, and others.
ECB rate hike impact on European economy.
US housing data and mortgage rate trends.
Regulatory outcomes for private credit and AI.
watch_list: 10-year Treasury yield (key level: 4.5%+ could trigger further selloff).
WTI crude oil price (above $90 signals sustained geopolitical risk).
Cerebras (CERE) stock performance as AI sentiment barometer.
Berkshire Hathaway 13F filings for further portfolio clues.
China economic data and private sector support measures.
Silver import rules in India and commodity hoarding trends.
SpaceX IPO filing status (potential market catalyst).
